sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.

Valid actions right now: MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Clicking the white cross moved the purple square from (58,3) to (53,10) - the cross's position. The cross disappeared. The purple square is now on the diagonal path. No new cross appeared. The bottom white bar shrank from 64 to 62 pixels (likely a step counter). The goal is to move the purple square along the path toward the blue creature.
- Goal model: Move the purple square all the way along the diagonal path to reach the blue creature at the top-right.
- Action model: Clicking the cross moved the purple square there. Need to figure out how to continue moving it - perhaps click on the purple square itself, or a new cross will appear.
- Plan: Try clicking on the purple square (now at ~53,10) to see if it advances further along the path.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 2 of about 22 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on the purple square at (53,10) again didn't move it - the purple square is still at the same position. The only thing that changed is the bottom bar went from 62 to 60 pixels. So clicking on the purple square itself just decrements the bar without moving the square.
This means I need to find a different approach. Let me think about what I know:
1. First click on the white cross at (53,10) moved the purple square from (58,3) to (53,10)
2. Second click on the same position (now the purple square) didn't move it, just decremented the bar
3. The bar went: 64 → 62 → 60 (decreases by 2 each action)
So the white cross was the key - it was a target marker. Now that it's gone and replaced by the purple square, I need to figure out how to continue.
Looking at the animation from the second click, there were multiple objects animated. Let me check if maybe a new cross appeared somewhere. But from the segmentation, there are no small white objects.
Wait - maybe I need to click on a gray dot on the path to move the purple square to that position? Or maybe I need to click on the NEXT position along the path?
The path goes from bottom-left to top-right. The purple square is currently at (53,10). The next gray dot toward the blue creature would be... let me think about the path direction.
The gray dots go: (57,6), (55,8), (51,12), (49,14), ..., (17,46)
The purple square is at (53,10), which is between (55,8) and (51,12).
If the purple square needs to move toward the blue creature (top-right), the next position would be (51,12). Let me try clicking on that gray dot.
Actually wait - looking at the image again, I see the purple square is now on the path. Maybe I need to click on the NEXT gray dot along the path to move the purple square there. The next dot toward the blue creature would be (51,12).
Let me try clicking on (51, 12).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking the purple square at its current position doesn't move it - only the bottom bar decreased (62→60). The white cross was a one-time target marker. Need to find how to advance the purple square further along the path.
Goal model: Move the purple square along the diagonal path toward the blue creature.
Action model: Clicking the cross moved the purple square there. Clicking the square itself just wastes a step. Need to try clicking on the next path position.
Plan: Try clicking on the next gray dot along the path toward the blue creature - position (51, 12).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the next gray dot along the path (toward blue creature)
# The path goes: (57,6) -> (55,8) -> [purple square at 53,10] -> (51,12) -> ...
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 12}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
